title: Iran announces that the death toll since the start of the airstrikes has risen to 787.

The Fujairah Government Media Office reported that authorities in the Emirate of Fujairah dealt with a fire that broke out this morning in the Fujairah Petroleum Industrial Estate (FOIZ). The fire was caused by falling debris following the successful interception of a drone by air defenses. No injuries were reported. The fire was brought under control and operations have resumed.

A top member of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' Quds Force who worked with Hezbollah was killed in an Israeli Navy strike in Beirut yesterday, the IDF announces. The military says the strike in the Lebanese capital killed Reza Khazaei, who headed the IRGC Quds Force's efforts to assist Hezbollah with rebuilding its capabilities. Khazaei also served as chief of staff at the Quds Force's Lebanon Corps, according to the IDF.
Iranian media: 13 Revolutionary Guards killed in US-Israeli attacks on Kerman province
Several loud explosions were heard in Khomein city in Markazi province on Tuesday morning.
French Foreign Minister: France is ready to defend its partners if they request assistance.
Israeli army: We are ready for an operation in Iran that will last for weeks
Violent explosions in Tehran
Iranian media: Massive explosion rocks Isfahan, airstrikes target areas east of Tehran
The U.S. has ordered non-emergency personnel and their families to leave Bahrain, Jordan, and Iraq
US Central Command: We targeted Iranian missile and drone launch sites
US Central Command: We destroyed Revolutionary Guard command and control centers

The Iranian Revolutionary Guard: Our naval forces targeted the Sheikh Isa US base in Bahrain at dawn with missiles and drones. We destroyed the main command and staff building at the US base in Bahrain with 20 drones and 3 missiles. Our attack on the American air base in Bahrain resulted in setting fire to its fuel tanks

There is footage on social networks claiming that an Iranian drone struck a building in Dubai. Earlier, the IRGC took responsibility for striking a site that housed American soldiers in Dubai. This footage may be related to the claim
Video shows an Iranian one-way attack drone being successfully intercepted tonight by a U.S. Army C-RAM at Erbil International Airport in Kurdistan, Northern Iraq
Saudi Ministry of Defense spokesperson: Eight drones intercepted and destroyed near Riyadh and Al-Kharj.

Sources report hearing explosions as Iranian airstrikes are intercepted over Manama.

Several flights to UAE, Saudi Arabia diverted, FlightRadar reports
Trump to News Nation: We are very close to achieving our goals in Iran

Netanyahu to Fox News: Iran's nuclear program would have become bomb-proof within months
A drone strike was reported on the King Fahd Causeway, the bridge linking Saudi Arabia and Bahrain across the Gulf - Picture sent to Faytuks Network by a local
Reuters, citing two sources: Two new explosions heard in Riyadh's diplomatic quarter
Iraqi factions claim responsibility for a drone attack on a hotel housing US troops in Erbil
NewsNation reports it spoke with President Trump, who said the U.S. retaliation for the attack on the U.S. Embassy in Riyadh and the killing of U.S. service members is coming "soon”
There are reports of additional airstrikes occurring in Khorramshahr, Iran
Scenes from the US embassy in Riyadh after it was targeted by an Iranian drone.
Saudi Ministry of Defense:  "The U.S. Embassy in Riyadh was subjected to an attack by two drones according to initial estimates, resulting in a limited fire and minor material damage to the building
At least 17 U.S Air Force Tankers are en route to the Middle East

The U.S. Embassy in Saudi Arabia has issued a shelter-in-place warning for all Americans in Dhahran, Jeddah, and Riyadh, the location of the Embassy, which is reported to have struck within the last hour by at least two drones launched by Iran
Australian Defence Minister: Al Minhad Air Base in Dubai, where Australian forces are stationed, was attacked by Iran earlier this week
UAE air defenses intercepted Iranian missiles, according to the UAE Ministry of Defense
Airstrikes target the IRGC’s Amir al-Mu'minin Brigade in Ilam, western Iran
Axios: President Trump spoke by phone with Kurdish leaders in Iraq on Sunday to discuss the U.S.-Israel war with Iran and what might come next, three sources with knowledge of the call told
Ilam, Ilam Province: Provincial Intelligence HQ has been reportedly targeted @ ~ 1900Z (March 2nd 2026)
5 explosions were heard from Kashan, eyewitness reports it was from the "Kashan Missile City", Isfahan Province, Iran
White House talking points for congressional Republicans say we're not at war, just "major combat operations against Iran"
Kuwaiti Army: We are dealing with a wave of detected missiles and drones to ensure the protection of the country's airspace.
Qatar: Our stockpiles of interceptor missiles have not been depleted and we maintain sufficient reserves.
New rocket alerts across Northern Israel

The US is preparing for a “major uptick” in attacks in Iran over the next 24 hours, a senior US official tells. The US has assessed that a first round of attacks has achieved the goal of weakening Iranian defenses, and the next phase will heavily focus on destroying the country’s missile production, unmanned aerial vehicles and naval capability, the official said. President Donald Trump previewed escalating attacks in an interview with CNN earlier Monday, saying, “We haven’t even started hitting them hard. The big wave hasn’t even happened. The big one is coming soon.”
An explosion was heard in the Iranian city of Qom.
UAE Ministry of Defense: We affirm our complete readiness to confront all threats to ensure the protection of the country's territory and the safety of its residents.

It appears to be an interception of Iranian missiles. A series of intense airstrikes targeted the outskirts of the Lebanese town of Hermel; the sounds of the shelling were clearly audible in the city of Qusayr.
Impacts in several areas of Israel as result of Iranian missile strikes, falling debris of intercepted missiles
UAE Ministry of Defense: Our air defenses are dealing with a barrage of ballistic missiles coming from Iran
Israeli air defenses intercept missiles in Tel Aviv
Air defenses worked in Kuwait
Airstrike on Punak neighborhood of Zanjan, Zanjan Province, Iran
Interceptions above Haifa

Interceptor missiles launched and explosions heard in the skies over Haifa
Israeli army: We are working to intercept new missiles launched from Iran
Iranian rocket barrage towards Beersheba and southern Israel
Explosion in Laar, Fars Province, Iran. Several sources report that the "Missile City" of Laar, aka IRGC ASF AB was hit, reportedly for the 7th time
Rocket alerts in Israel in Upper Galilee, Center Galilee
After a lull of some seven hours, a new launch of ballistic missiles from Iran has been detected by the Israeli army. Sirens are expected to sound in northern Israel shortly
The Quneitra (Dezful) Fourth Air Base in Ahvaz, one of the most important bases of the Iranian Air Force, is being heavily bombed.
Reports of explosions in Cyprus
Fox News, citing US Central Command: The Strait of Hormuz is not closed.

President Donald Trump said on Monday his decision to withdraw from the 2015 Iran nuclear deal prevented Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon, calling the agreement “the most dangerous transaction” ever made. "If I didn’t terminate Obama’s horrendous Iran Nuclear Deal (JCPOA), Iran would have had a Nuclear Weapon three years ago. That was the most dangerous transaction we have ever entered into, and had it been allowed to stand, the World would be an entirely different place right now. You can blame Barack Hussein Obama, and Sleepy Joe Biden," Trump said
The White House, in a memo to Republican lawmakers in Congress, stated: Our military operation aims to end the threat posed by Iran's proxies.
Smoke rose from two explosions near the state broadcaster's headquarters in Tehran.
Following the Israeli airstrikes, Iranian television says its broadcasts are continuing normally.
Israeli army: We destroyed the Iranian television headquarters in a raid on Tehran

Natanz has been hit. Satellite images show that two entrances used by personnel at the Natanz nuclear facility's underground enrichment complex and another entrance used by vehicles have been severely damaged

Hezbollah’s Al-Manar TV interrupted its live broadcast and is currently airing clips praising Khamenei, with a stripe reading: “The Israeli army threatens to strike a building in southern Beirut.” The expected target is Hezbollah’s Al-Manar TV building in Beirut’s Dahiyeh
Tasnim News Agency: Smoke rises from two explosions near the television building
Iranian State TV says that the Iranian State Broadcasting Headquarters (IRIB) were targeted in a U.S./Israeli airstrike

The @SecRubio @StateDept urges Americans to DEPART NOW from the countries below using available commercial transportation, due to serious safety risks. From: Bahrain, Kuwait, Egypt, Lebanon, Iran, Oman, Iraq, Qatar, Israel, the West Bank and Gaza, Saudi Arabia, Syria, United Arab Emirates, Jordan, Yemen
The Jordanian army: Our position is firm and Jordan will not be a battleground for anyone.
A loud explosion was heard in Tehran.
The Israeli army announces the start of a new wave of strikes on Tehran.

UAE says it has detected 174 ballistic missiles fired at it from Iran, intercepted 161. The rest fell in the sea. It intercepted all 8 cruise missiles. Out of 689 UAV’s fired at UAE, 44 Iranian drones hit UAE territory
Tasnim News Agency: The latest airstrikes targeted the vicinity of the old parliament building in Tehran.

The Islamic Resistance in Iraq, a grouping of militias and front organizations backed by Iran, published footage of missile and drone launches against what it says are American targets in the country and the region
Israeli aircraft have just shot down a drone over the al-Mseifra area, east of Daraa
Two protesters were killed during demonstrations against Washington's war on Iran near the US embassy in Bahrain.

The IDF issues an "urgent warning" to Iranians in Tehran's Evin neighborhood, ahead of planned airstrikes on Iranian military infrastructure. "Urgent warning to all individuals present in the Evin area in Tehran, within the vicinity of the broadcasting authority, according to the area marked in red on the attached map," says the IDF's Persian-language spokesman, Lt. Col. Kamal Penhasi in a statement.

Secretary Rubio: "They're suffering a tremendous amount of damage. Honestly, again I'm not going to give away the details of our tactical efforts, but hardest hits are yet to come from the U.S. military. The next phase will be even more punishing on Iran than it is right now."

CENTCOM: – As of 4 pm ET, March 2, six U.S. service members have been killed in action. U.S. forces recently recovered the remains of two previously unaccounted for service members from a facility that was struck during Iran's initial attacks in the region. Major combat operations continue.
IRGC: 13th wave of Operation Sadeq Promise 4 has begun

The IRGC-affiliated Fars News reported multiple explosions in Tehran, saying initial reports indicate the parliament building has been targeted in airstrikes on Monday evening. It said it is investigating those reports
Bahraini military reinforcements are heading towards the US embassy. There are fears in the area of violent protests that could attempt to storm the embassy
Air raid sirens are sounding in Jordan minutes after the U.S. Embassy in Amman was evacuated over a “security threat.” The Jordanian government has urged residents near the embassy to take shelter

Rubio speaking to reporters now says the “imminent threat” the administration has referred to to justify US strikes on Iran was that they knew Israel was going to attack Tehran, and believed such an attack would prompt Iran to strike US bases and assets in the region
Kuwaiti General Staff: We mourn the loss of a second member of the naval force while performing his duty
Advisor to the commander of Iran's Revolutionary Guard: We will target  oil pipelines and will not allow oil exports from the region.
Advisor to the commander of Iran's Revolutionary Guard: The Strait of Hormuz is closed and we will target any ship that tries to pass through.

Heavy shelling targeted the Iranian army's airbase in Quneitra (Dezful) in Ahvaz.
Araghchi: U.S. bases remain legitimate targets even if they are not used against us
US military: We have struck 1,250 targets in Iran since the start of the operation
